back_translation: And Elihu continued:
'Bear with me a little longer, and I will show you that there is still more to say on God's behalf.
I will bring my knowledge from afar and ascribe righteousness to my Maker.
For truly my words are not false; one perfect in knowledge is with you.
Behold, God is mighty, but He despises no one; He is mighty in strength of understanding.
He does not keep the wicked alive, but grants justice to the afflicted.
He sets the righteous with kings on the throne and exalts them forever, and He never withdraws His eyes from them.
And if people are bound with chains, caught in cords of affliction,
then He shows them their deeds and the transgressions they committed in arrogance.
He opens their ears to correction and commands them to turn from iniquity.
If they obey and serve Him, they will end their days in prosperity and their years in happiness.
But if they do not obey, they will perish by the sword and die without knowledge.
The godless in heart store up resentment; even when He binds them, they do not cry for help.
They die in their youth, their life ending among the male shrine prostitutes.
God rescues the afflicted through their affliction and opens their ears in oppression.
Indeed, He drew you from the jaws of distress to a broad place without limits, and to a table full of rich food.
But now you are filled with the judgment due the wicked; judgment and justice have seized you.
Beware that no one entices you with riches, and do not let a large bribe lead you astray.
Will your wealth or all your strength keep you from distress?
Do not long for the night, when people are taken away from their place.
Beware, do not turn to iniquity, for you seem to have preferred this to affliction.
Behold, God is exalted in His power. Who is a teacher like Him?
Who has appointed His way for Him, or said to Him, 'You have done wrong'?
Remember to magnify His work, which men have praised in song.
All mankind has seen His work; people gaze at it from afar.
Behold, God is great beyond our knowledge; the number of His years is unsearchable.
For He draws up the drops of water, which distill into rain from the mist,
which the clouds pour out and shower abundantly on mankind.
Moreover, who can understand how the clouds spread out, and how the thunder roars from His pavilion?
Behold, He scatters His lightning around Him and covers the depths of the sea.
For by these He judges the nations and provides food in abundance.
He holds the lightning in His hands and commands it to strike its target.
The thunder announces His coming; even the herds sense the rising storm.
